INT. COFFEE SHOP - DAY

SARAH sits across from JAMES at a small table. Two cups of coffee steam between them.

SARAH
So you're really leaving?

JAMES
The flight's tomorrow morning.

SARAH
Just like that. No discussion. No— nothing?

JAMES
What is there to discuss? I got the position. It's what I've been working toward.

SARAH
For three years. And in three years you never once mentioned moving to London.

JAMES
I didn't think I'd get it.

SARAH
But you applied.

JAMES
Of course I applied. Who wouldn't?

SARAH
Someone who has a life here. Someone who has— people.

JAMES looks down at his coffee. A long silence.

JAMES
I'm sorry, Sarah. I truly am.

SARAH
Sorry doesn't fix this. Sorry doesn't undo the last three years.

JAMES
No. But it's all I've got.

SARAH stands up, grabs her coat.

SARAH
Then I guess that's not enough.

She turns and walks out. JAMES sits alone, staring at the empty chair across from him.

CUT TO:

INT. JAMES'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

JAMES paces the room, phone pressed to his ear.

JAMES
(into phone)
I know. I know. But what was I supposed to do?

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
You were supposed to talk to her first.

JAMES
And say what? "Hey, I might be moving across the world, but let's not panic"?

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
Yeah. That. Exactly that.

JAMES stops pacing. He looks at a framed photo on the shelf — him and SARAH at a beach, laughing.

JAMES
I messed this up, didn't I?

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
Big time.

FADE OUT.
